subject: deep-session-agent-count
insight: Deep sessions perform well with 5-18 agents across 4-5 waves (avg 10 agents, 4.5 waves). Sessions with fewer agents (5-8) still complete successfully but touch fewer files.
evidence: 12 deep sessions: agents=[9,17,18,15,7,13,7,5,8,6,14,16], waves=[4,4,5,5,5,5,4,4,5,4,5,5]. All achieved 100% completion except Session 3 (04-07): 25 agents, 8 waves, 76%. Session 04-12 v5: 16 agents, 5 waves, 16 files, 100%.

subject: single-issue-focus
insight: Planning 1 issue per deep session yields 100% completion reliably. The codebase supports focused single-issue sessions with no carryover.
evidence: 6 deep sessions with effectiveness data all planned 1 issue (or 0+1 emergent) and achieved 100% completion with 0 carryover.

subject: batch-issue-sessions
insight: Batch sessions of 3-14 cohesive issues achieve 100% completion when issues are independent. 1 agent per issue parallelizes perfectly. Works across feature, deep, and housekeeping session types.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-06-1029: 3 issues (same file), 8 agents. Session main-2026-04-06-1830: 3 independent issues, 6 agents. Session main-2026-04-06-housekeeping3: 4 cross-repo cleanup tasks, 4 agents. Session main-2026-04-07-housekeeping: 5 issues, 5 agents, 1 wave, 100% completion. Session main-2026-04-07-2310: 3 issues, 3 agents, 2 waves, 100%. Session main-2026-04-07-1600: 14 issues, 11 agents, 5 waves, 100% — largest successful batch.

subject: cross-repo-cleanup-sizing
insight: Cross-repo housekeeping (git-rm, file deletion, config updates) works well with 3-4 agents and coordinator-driven checkpoints. Destructive operations need explicit user confirmation gates between waves.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-06-housekeeping3: BuchhaltGenie cleanup, 4 agents across 3 waves, 1425 files changed, 100% completion. User checkpoint before git-rm prevented accidental data loss.

subject: simplification-pass-catches-ai-slop
insight: Simplification pass consistently catches real dead code and over-documentation from AI-generated refactoring. AI agents produce slop comments, unnecessary try-catch, and redundant logic that a targeted cleanup pass removes.
evidence: Session 5 (04-06): caught dead code and over-docs in quality-gate refactor. Session 8 (04-06): removed AI slop comments from both bash scripts. Session 2 (04-04): simplification pass was highest-impact finding. Session 04-12 v4: found 1 redundant clause in session-end. Session 04-12 v5: 11 slop fixes across 9 files — removed redundant paragraphs, duplicate platform notes, verbose examples.

subject: discovery-false-positive-rate
insight: Discovery probes have a ~50-60% noise rate (false positives + low-value findings). Agent findings must always be verified against source code before triaging to the user. Auto-deferring low-confidence findings and dual filtering (agent + coordinator) significantly reduces noise.
evidence: Session 5 (04-04): 8/13 = 62% false positives. Session 6 (04-06): 6/8 probes clean, only 2 real findings. Session 4 (04-06): 1 auto-deferred, 3/4 confirmed after verification. Session housekeeping (04-07): 11 raw, 9 verified, 4 dismissed = 55% noise.

subject: version-string-drift
insight: Version strings drift across docs files (marketplace.json, CONTRIBUTING.md, USER-GUIDE.md, README badge) between releases. A grep check for the old version string should be part of every finalization wave. Also check Session Config test-command path when test directories move.
evidence: Session 4 (04-04): 3 stale version strings. Session 3 (04-06): marketplace.json lagged. Session 6 (04-06): version audit confirmed all aligned only after explicit check. Session housekeeping (04-09): 5 stale strings (mcp-server.sh, USER-GUIDE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, marketplace.json, test fixture) + broken test-command path.

subject: lifecycle-simulation-finds-instruction-gaps
insight: Code-level lifecycle simulation (reading every skill and tracing data flow end-to-end) finds instruction gaps and contradictions that manual testing and running the system miss. Most effective for plugin/orchestration repos where 'running tests' doesn't cover instruction correctness.
evidence: v1 (04-04): 15 issues. v2 (04-06): 17 items, 9 fixed. v3 (04-07): 37 gaps, 28 fixed. v4 (04-12): 26 gaps, 24 fixed. v5 (04-12): 64 findings (13 CRITICAL, 20 HIGH) across wave planning pipeline, cross-skill data contracts, /plan skill, learning lifecycle, platform paths, and commands. 44 fixed in 16 files.

subject: platform-abstraction-skill-layer-gap
insight: Scripts (platform.sh, enforce-scope.sh) handle all 3 platforms correctly, but Markdown skill instructions and probe code hardcode .claude/ paths. After adding a new platform or running a lifecycle sim, a dedicated pass to check ALL skill body text and probe scripts is required.
evidence: v3 (04-07): 25+ hardcoded .claude/ refs. v4 (04-12): hardcoded .claude/ in probes-session.md. v5 (04-12): 10 genuine issues found (2 CRITICAL, 4 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM) across session-end, session-start, wave-executor, config-reading, plan/mode-retro, probes-session. All fixed with <state-dir> placeholders and platform gates.

subject: plugin-manifest-zod-validation
insight: plugin.json path fields (agents, commands, hooks, mcpServers) cause Zod validation failure if set to directory paths. Claude Code auto-discovers agents/, commands/, hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json — never specify these fields. Agent frontmatter: tools as comma-separated string (not JSON array), description as inline single-line string (not YAML block scalar >). Always validate with /doctor before release.
evidence: Session 2026-04-08: 'agents: ./agents' caused 'agents: Invalid input' — Zod expects .md file paths not directories. Took 5 iterations to find root cause. Binary analysis revealed iz6()=Qn().endsWith('.md'). All working plugins (superpowers, feature-dev, pr-review-toolkit) use metadata-only plugin.json with auto-discovery.

subject: feature-session-sizing
insight: Feature sessions scale linearly with issue count: 3-11 agents across 2-5 waves with consistent 100% completion. Larger feature batches (14 issues, 11 agents, 5 waves) complete just as reliably as small ones (3 issues, 3 agents, 2 waves).
evidence: Session main-2026-04-07-1600: 14 issues, 11 agents, 5 waves, 32 files, 100% completion. Session main-2026-04-07-2310: 3 issues, 3 agents, 2 waves, 3 files, 100% completion.

subject: housekeeping-single-wave
insight: Housekeeping sessions work best with single-wave execution and 0-6 agents. The lightweight structure fits their nature — quick fixes, version audits, config cleanup. No benefit from multi-wave planning.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-07-2100: 1 wave, 6 agents, 24 files, 5+3 emergent issues, 100% completion. Session main-2026-04-09-1200: 1 wave, 0 agents, 6 files, coordinator-only, 100% completion. Session 04-12 housekeeping: coordinator-only analysis + inline fixes, no wave dispatch needed. Session 04-12 session 4: single commit, coordinator-only CI fix + release. 5/5 housekeeping sessions confirmed single-wave.

subject: inter-skill-data-flow-analysis
insight: Tracing config propagation across skill boundaries (session-start to session-plan to wave-executor) reveals broken data chains that neither lifecycle simulation nor tests catch. Complementary to lifecycle sim: sim traces individual skill instructions, data-flow analysis traces the PIPELINE between skills.
evidence: Session 04-12 housekeeping: 4 systematic gaps found. Session 04-12 v5: explicitly traced 8 data contracts across session-plan→wave-executor, discovery→session-end, session-end→evolve. Found 1 CRITICAL broken contract (Execution Config missing persistence+pencil), 2 CRITICAL schema mismatches (discovery findings fields, issue-creation gap). All fixed. Independent confirmation across 2 sessions strengthens pattern.

subject: new-skills-need-lifecycle-sim-before-release
insight: New skills (/plan with 3 modes) that skip lifecycle simulation accumulate instruction gaps rapidly. First audit of /plan found 20 findings (3 CRITICAL, 7 HIGH) including hardcoded paths, missing platform fallbacks, priority label mismatches, and undefined edge cases. Lifecycle sim should be part of the definition-of-done for new skills.
evidence: Session 04-12 v5: /plan skill had NEVER been lifecycle-simmed despite being released in alpha.7. First audit found 20 findings across SKILL.md, mode-new.md, mode-feature.md, mode-retro.md. Critical issues included hardcoded ~/.claude/ path in agent prompt, P0/P1 vs priority:critical label mismatch, inconsistent VCS CLI syntax.

subject: lifecycle-sim-effectiveness-untracked
insight: Lifecycle simulation sessions produce 24-44 real fixes but report planned_issues:0, completed:0 in effectiveness tracking. Sim findings are not VCS issues, so the effectiveness metric sees these as empty sessions. This creates a blind spot: the most impactful deep sessions appear unproductive in metrics.
evidence: Session main-2026-04-12-0824 (v4): 14 agents, 10 files changed, 24 gaps fixed — effectiveness: {planned:0, completed:0}. Session main-2026-04-12-1230 (v5): 16 agents, 16 files changed, 44 findings fixed — effectiveness: {planned:0, completed:0}. Both sessions had 100% quality pass across all waves.

subject: ci-environment-assumptions
insight: Tests that pass locally can fail in CI due to missing environment setup (git user.name/email, tool versions, env vars). CI runners start from a bare environment — any implicit dependency on local config is a latent failure. Guard setup steps (git config, tool checks) should be part of test fixtures.
evidence: Session 04-12 session 4: test-worktree.sh failed in GitHub Actions because temp repo had no git user.name/email configured. First green CI since April 7 (5 days broken). Fix: added git config to test setup block.
